sources.notes: Statement and constraints: fill an n by m grid with numbers 1..k equally often, with no equal edge-adjacent cells; k >= 2 and k divides n*m.
Official editorial source for Codeforces Round 1017; used to confirm the constructive coloring approach.
Maspy writeup gives the key construction: take a prime factor p of k, use a_{i+j}=(i+j) mod p, then replace each residue x by x, x+p, x+2p, ... to distribute all k labels.

annotation.summary: Construct an n by m grid using labels 1..k equally often so that edge-adjacent cells never have the same label.
annotation.constraints: 2 <= n*m <= 2e5, 2 <= k <= n*m, and k divides n*m. The total grid area over test cases is <= 2e5.
annotation.core_idea: Pick a prime factor p of k. Since k divides n*m, p divides n or m. Color the grid by residue (i+j) mod p. If p divides m, every row contains each residue exactly m/p times; if p divides n, every column does, so each residue class has exactly n*m/p cells. Adjacent cells change the residue by 1 mod p, hence they cannot receive labels from the same residue class. Split labels into p groups by their value modulo p: r, r+p, r+2p, ... . While scanning the cells of residue r, cycle through that group's labels evenly. Because n*m/k is an integer, each of the k labels appears exactly n*m/k times.
annotation.complexity: O(nm) time and O(k) auxiliary counters
tricks: Use a prime factor of k so it must divide at least one grid dimension.
Color cells by diagonal residue (i+j) mod p to separate adjacent cells.
Distribute labels inside each residue class by cycling values congruent modulo p.

solution_variants.name: prime-factor-residue-grid
solution_variants.summary: Choose a prime p dividing k. Use p residue classes on the grid by (i+j) mod p, then assign labels with the same residue modulo p cyclically inside each class.

tags.evidence: The task only asks for any valid grid, and the solution explicitly constructs one from modular residue classes.
The grid is first colored by (i+j) mod p so edge-adjacent cells always belong to different color classes.
tags.description: Use a coloring of grid cells, often by parity or modular residues, to enforce local adjacency constraints.
tags.created_reason: Existing constructive and residue-class tags do not explicitly capture grid adjacency coloring patterns.

tags.evidence: Both cells and labels are partitioned by residues modulo p, and labels are assigned within matching residue classes.
The construction relies on choosing a prime p|k and using k|nm to conclude p divides n or m.
The pattern is periodic with period p along rows and columns.
